responseMessageContents.text:
I can see the code is actually at version `v3.0.1` in `_layout.tsx`, but the settings page still hardcodes "Version 1.0.0". The preview IS showing the latest code — the "1.0.0" you see is just a hardcoded string in the settings page, not an indication of an old version.

Let me update the version display in settings to match the actual app version, and also make a small visible change to help you confirm the preview is current.

The preview **is** showing your latest code. The "Version 1.0.0" you saw was just a hardcoded string in the settings page — not an indicator of which version of your codebase is running.

I've updated it to show **Version 3.0.1** to match your actual app version. Refresh the preview and you should see "Version 3.0.1" in the About row, confirming everything is up to date.

responseMessageContents.title: Updated the version number shown in the settings menu.
responseMessageContents.description: The version number displayed in the settings was changed from 1.0.0 to 3.0.1 so that it correctly shows the current version of the app. This was just a display label and did not affect how the app works.
